FIXER: Mark 16.2 complete [P][C][T][F] — all tests pass, no implementation fixes needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Arc::relocated() storage corruption when source == destination (#310)

`Arc::relocated()` corrupts shared storage when `source == destination`:
it stores the factory-created value at the destination slot, then
unconditionally overwrites that same slot with the stale pre-relocation
value. Any subsequent `relocated()` call hits the `get_clone` fast-path
and silently returns the wrong data.

## Changes

- **Bug fix**: Guard the source-slot restore with `if source !=
destination` — skips the overwrite when both affinities refer to the
same slot.
- **Regression test**:
`test_relocated_source_equals_destination_does_not_corrupt_storage` —
advances a counter to 42, relocates with `source == destination`
(factory resets to 0), then performs a second relocation; without the
fix the second call would return 42 via the corrupted fast-path.

```rust
// Before fix: storage at `affinity` ends up holding the stale Counter(42),
// so the second relocated() returns 42 instead of 0.
let arc = PerCore::new(Counter::new);
arc.increment_by(42);

let relocated = arc.relocated(affinity.into(), affinity); // value = 0 (new), storage = 42 (stale) ← bug
let again = relocated.relocated(affinity.into(), affinity); // returns 42 ← wrong

// After fix: storage holds 0, second call returns 0.
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Improve the soundness of relocating thread_aware::Arc
when multiple sources are provided</issue_title>
> <issue_description>I have encountered an issue when relocating
`thread_aware::Arc` and unknowingly providing multiple different source
affinities. This caused a bug where I had a "dead instance" caused by
multiple relocations.
> 
> The problem disappeared where I made sure that only a single source
affinity was provided across all relocations calls. Would be nice to
provide some debug assertion to ensure caller provides a proper source
affinity.
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> <comment_new><author>@Darksecond</author><body>
> Bug: thread_aware::Arc::relocated() corrupts storage when source ==
destination
> 
> In Arc::relocated() (cell/mod.rs line 488), when the source and
destination affinities refer to the same slot, the method:
> 
> 1. Creates a new value via factory and stores it at the destination
slot (line 524)
> 2. Unconditionally stores the pre-relocation self.value at the source
slot (line 534) — which is the same slot — overwriting the value just
created in step 1
> 
> The returned Arc correctly holds the factory-created value, but the
shared storage now holds the stale pre-relocation value. Any subsequent
relocated() call
> targeting that affinity finds the stale value via
get_clone(destination) (line 492), silently returning the wrong data.
> 
>   Fix: Skip the source store when source equals destination:
> 
>    if let MemoryAffinity::Pinned(source) = source {
>        if source != destination {
>            guard.replace(source, self.value);
>        }
>    }
> 
> Workaround: Callers can pass MemoryAffinity::Unknown as source to
bypass the source-store path entirely (the if let Pinned guard is false
for Unknown).</body></comment_new>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

feat: retry partial collections after rate-limit cool-down

Add a second sequential pass for partial collections after
the main concurrent sweep. When Fragment rate-limits specific
collection endpoints during the parallel phase (concurrency=4),
those collections are retried one-by-one after a 15s cool-down
(SCRAPER_PARTIAL_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS) using fresh sessions.

This eliminates the remaining ~70 partial for_sale collections
without requiring proxy rotation or lower overall concurrency.

commit.message:
Clean up rewards after PBRS implementation (#163)

* Tune rewards to encourage room exploration

Three changes to fix the model stalling at room 5 (0x38):

1. Reduce wrong-location penalty: -1.00 → -0.50
   The -1.00 cliff made exploring unknown exits net-negative (50% chance
   of -1.00 vs 50% chance of +0.75). At -0.50, trying new exits has
   positive expected value (+0.125), encouraging exploration.

2. Increase new-location reward: +0.75 → +1.00
   One successful room discovery should decisively outweigh penalties
   accumulated during exploration attempts.

3. Remove sword miss penalty entirely (-0.06/miss)
   Misses already cost time (animation lock + tile revisit penalties).
   The flat miss penalty made the model risk-averse about swinging,
   which hurts combat learning especially early in training. Removes
   torch dependency from critics.py.

* Sort model files by step count descending in debugger browser

Models with no step count appear first, followed by highest to lowest
step count. Previously used alphabetical order which didn't match
numerical checkpoint ordering.

* Fix diagnose.py to use pbrs_wavefront for PBRS diagnostic

The --infinite-pbrs mode was using prev.wavefront (includes items) instead
of prev.pbrs_wavefront (item-free, matches critics.py). This caused false
non-stationarity warnings from dropped items. Also removes unused
ObjectiveKind import.

* Enable key usage reward for dungeon progression

Uncomment critique_used_key (+0.25 for using a key at a locked door).
The model in dungeon1-skip-opening reaches the locked north door in room
0x63 at distance 4 but has no signal to walk through it despite having
3 keys. This provides the missing incentive.

* Lower tile revisit threshold from 8 to 4

Threshold 8 was tuned for the old system which had a -0.01/step
asymmetry on every move. Without that baseline cost, large rooms
(50+ tiles) let the model distribute visits broadly enough to stay
under threshold indefinitely. At threshold 4, penalties start around
step ~200 and escalate, making prolonged wandering expensive without
punishing short-term backtracking.

This is a separate commit for easy rollback if too aggressive.

* Remove dynamic LR adjustment, use fixed learning rate

The _adjust_learning_rate method had a bug where it read room-result
(an EnumMetric) instead of success-rate, causing LR to drop to minimum
at 0% actual success. Rather than fix the heuristic, remove it entirely
in favor of a fixed LR (0.0001). LR scheduling can be revisited later
with a proper approach (linear decay, cosine, etc.).

Removed: _adjust_learning_rate(), LEARNING_RATE_LOW, LEARNING_RATE_MINIMUM,
dynamic_lr kwarg, --high-lr CLI flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add small sword miss penalty (-0.01) when enemies present

Swinging the sword and missing when enemies are in the room now incurs
a REWARD_MINIMUM (-0.01) penalty. This gives slight negative pressure
to discourage flailing without meaningfully punishing exploration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove room 0x73 restriction from dungeon1-skip-opening LeftPlayArea

Only end the scenario when the agent actually leaves dungeon 1 (level != 1),
not when it enters room 0x73 (one tile south of start).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove ATTACK_NO_ENEMIES_PENALTY (sword masked when no enemies)

Sword actions are already masked off when no enemies are present,
making this penalty dead code. The miss penalty (-0.01) remains
for swings that miss when enemies are nearby.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace tile revisit penalty with room step stalling penalty

The per-tile revisit penalty was exploitable by looping through many
distinct tiles. Replace with a room-level step counter: after 150
steps in the same room, penalty escalates as -0.01 * excess/50.
Counter resets on room change, enemy kills (fewer active enemies),
or item pickups.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add -5.0 terminal penalty on timeout (bypasses reward clamp)

When an episode is truncated (timeout), apply a -5.0 penalty that
bypasses the per-step [-1, 1] reward clamp. This ensures GAE propagates
a strong negative signal backward through the episode, preventing the
model from learning that stalling with positive rewards is acceptable.

Uses StepRewards.terminal_penalty field added to rewards.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Add -5.0 terminal penalty on timeout (bypasses reward clamp)"

This reverts commit 9cecea288be4e12ffee7393cc5a7d07421f318ed.

* Simplify Timeout: keep position-stuck and no-discovery only

Removed no_next_room_timeout (300 steps was too aggressive, cutting off
episodes before the room stalling penalty could do its job) and tile
revisit timeout (redundant with room step stalling penalty in critic).

Remaining timeouts:
- position_timeout=50: genuine stuck-in-wall detection
- no_discovery_timeout=2000: safety net for truly lost episodes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scale back room stalling penalty: flat -0.01 ramping to -0.02

Previous penalty escalated quadratically and dominated all other signals
(-40 to -120 per episode vs ~10 for PBRS). Replace with gentle linear
ramp: -0.01/step after 150 grace, slowly increasing to -0.02/step over
1850 steps. Max total penalty over full 2000 steps ~= -28 vs PBRS ~10.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix python path

* Tooltips

* Add tensorboard to requirements.txt

tensorflow 2.21.0 no longer bundles tensorboard as a transitive

* feat: replace unbounded sync.Map with configurable LRU parse cache

Replace the unbounded sync.Map parseCache with a bounded LRU cache
using container/list + sync.Mutex. Default capacity is 1000 entries.

New public API:
- ResetParseCache(): clears all cached entries
- SetParseCacheSize(n): configures max cache entries, evicts LRU if shrunk
- ParseCacheLen(): returns current number of cached entries

Tests cover LRU eviction order, cache reset, size configuration,
shrinking with eviction, and all previous cache behaviors.

  prevent duplicate parsing when multiple goroutines miss the cache for
  the same expression simultaneously.
- Fix BenchmarkEvaluateNoCache to call the parser directly, avoiding

commit.message:
fix(scripts): use AzureCliCredential in CI for generate.env.ts

In CI, azure/login authenticates as the Infrastructure UAMI via OIDC and
sets up the Azure CLI context. But AZURE_CLIENT_ID is set to the Frontend
UAMI, causing DefaultAzureCredential to try ManagedIdentityCredential
with the wrong identity (which doesn't exist in CI anyway).

Use AzureCliCredential directly in CI (detected via CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS env)
so the token is acquired through the azure/login OIDC session, which uses
the Infrastructure UAMI's federated credential.

commit.message:
cfl: fix patch path format for CFL-074, 075, 076 (CWE-400 timeout)

Three CWE-400 timeout patches (074, 075, 076) used plain paths instead of
git a/b/ format. build.sh uses `patch -p1` which strips the first path
component — with plain paths like `IccProfLib/X`, -p1 stripped the
directory prefix, causing all 3 patches to silently SKIP on every build.

Result: CheckUnderflowOverflow had depth=100 (should be 16) and no ops
budget, EvaluateProfile had no iteration cap, NamedColor2 had no nCoords
cap. All documented timeout protections were never active.

Fix: Convert all 3 patches to git `--- a/` / `+++ b/` format.
Verified: all 3 apply cleanly with -p1. Timeout artifacts that took 300+s
now complete in <30ms. 18/18 fuzzers rebuilt. 230/230 analyzer tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtimed): kill kernel processes on daemon shutdown (#664)

* fix(runtimed): kill kernel processes on daemon shutdown

Kernels are spawned with process_group(0) so they don't receive the
SIGINT/SIGTERM that hits the daemon. Previously the daemon relied on
RoomKernel's Drop impl to killpg the process groups, but Drop doesn't
reliably fire because:

1. RoomKernel is behind Arc<Mutex<Option<...>>> inside Arc<NotebookRoom>
   and multiple spawned tasks hold Arc clones that may not all unwind
   during tokio runtime teardown.
2. A second ctrl-c or SIGKILL skips destructors entirely.

Now we explicitly drain all notebook rooms and call kernel.shutdown()
(which sends ShutdownRequest + SIGKILL to the process group and removes
the connection file) before run() returns.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FDgQx2NBjRgtUxgcfqEt43

* Update daemon.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
